meaning: n.肌肉,精力,体力,原动力,[解]腱vt.赋予力量,加强
examples: He was tall and delicately shaped; strong in nerve and sinew, and most dextrous in arms.
All tough sinew, she looked like a hawk eyeing prey.
Prayer is the slender sinew that moves the muscle of omnipotence.
Lino is as thin as a whippet, all sinew and lean muscle, and dark with the sun.
Nixon can paint likable stage pictures with an effective emotional palette, but intellectual sinew and humor are not among her primary colors.
